title: Saving a Newcastle professional services firm $180,000 over 5 years through lease negotiation
excerpt: The firm was renewing their CBD lease and had already agreed to the landlord's first offer. We stepped in, renegotiated from scratch, and recovered $180,000 over the term.
challenge: A 12-person professional services firm in the Newcastle CBD was approaching lease renewal. They had a good relationship with the landlord and had already verbally agreed to a 5-year renewal at the current rent with a 4% annual increase. They engaged us to 'just check the paperwork'. What we found was that the agreed terms were significantly above market, the make-good clause required full reinstatement, and there was no fitout contribution despite the firm planning a full office refresh.
solution: We paused the process, conducted a thorough market analysis of comparable CBD tenancies, and presented the landlord's agent with a counter-proposal. We negotiated: face rent reduced to market rate (saving $28,000 p.a.), a $45,000 fitout contribution to fund the planned refresh, a 3-month rent-free period at commencement, CPI-capped rent reviews instead of fixed 4% increases, and make-good amended to fair wear and tear.
outcome: The firm signed a lease that was materially better across every commercial term. Total saving over the 5-year term: approximately $180,000 when including fitout contribution, reduced rent, rent-free, and avoided make-good liability. Our fee was $12,000.

quote.text: We thought we'd already done the deal. Joe looked at it and within a week had fundamentally changed every major term. The difference between what we were about to sign and what we actually signed was extraordinary.
